macro_events.event_summary: Energy stocks are leading the market in 2026 on Iran war-driven crude premium, but MarketWatch warns the war rally may be peaking and gains could erode from here.
primary_drivers: Crude oil war-risk premium potentially unwinding
Profit-taking in extended energy sector leadership
Demand destruction risk if oil stays elevated

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of airline operating costs; any unwind of the Iran war crude premium directly expands margins. Delta is most fuel-sensitive of the legacy carriers and has been pressured by 2026 energy outperformance.
Southwest has highest fuel cost as % of opex among majors and limited hedging vs peers; a crude pullback would disproportionately benefit its margin structure.
Cruise operators burn marine fuel heavily; bunker fuel costs track crude. A rollover in oil from Iran-war peak would be a direct COGS tailwind.
Dollar General's low-income customer is squeezed by gasoline prices; an oil pullback restores discretionary spend at the margin. Pure-play exposure to gas-price-sensitive consumer.

losers.rationale: Largest US integrated oil by market cap and a core holding driving 2026 energy sector leadership; if war premium erodes, XOM sees direct downside on both upstream realizations and sentiment-driven multiple compression.
Pure-play US E&P with high leverage to WTI/Brent realizations — no downstream buffer. Among the biggest 2026 outperformers that would give back gains on a crude rollover.
High-beta Permian producer with elevated leverage; earnings and equity most sensitive to crude price moves on the downside. Has benefited most from Iran premium.
Oilfield services capex is set by E&P cash flows which track crude; a sustained pullback pressures 2026 capex guides and SLB's international-weighted book.
North American completions activity is the most crude-price-sensitive part of OFS; Halliburton earnings lever directly to WTI levels that drive frac demand.
Permian pure-play with aggressive buybacks tied to oil-linked FCF; loses buyback capacity and multiple if crude premium deflates.

headlines: Energy stocks are trouncing the rest of the stock market in 2026. Why the Iran war could erode those gains from here.
